descriptions: The Ramashish Koeri gang cooperated with these four other gangs in committing murders.
edges.evidence_quote: The gang, along with four other caste-based gangs led by Ghamari Karwar, Rambachan Yadav, Triveni Kahar, and Gaurik was responsible for over hundred murders in the Kaimur region.

Rockford crime family.description: The Rockford crime family was an Italian American Mafia crime family based in Rockford, Illinois, founded around 1920 by Antonio Musso. It operated independently despite proximity to the Chicago Outfit, engaging in racketeering, gambling, loan sharking, and murder. The family was led by Joseph Zammuto from 1957 to 1974, then by Frank Buscemi until its dissolution around 2005.

descriptions: The Rockford family was independent but geographically close to the Chicago Outfit.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1960, the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) confirmed that the Rockford family had remained an independent crime family, even though they were within close distance to the powerful Chicago Outfit.

descriptions: In 1960, the FBI found little connection between the Rockford family and the Chicago Outfit.
edges.evidence_quote: It was concluded by the FBI that Ferraro did not know the boss of the Rockford family, confirming that there was little connection between the two crime families.

Markham Gang.description: The Markham Gang was a 19th-century criminal organization based in Ontario, Canada, evolving from groups supporting the Upper Canada Rebellion of 1837. Composed mainly of well-to-do farmers, it engaged in petty theft and counterfeiting, using a courier network to distribute stolen goods. The gang was broken up in 1846, with members receiving harsh sentences, though most were released within five years.

'Ndrina Avignone.description: The Avignone are a 'ndrina (clan) of the 'Ndrangheta, based in Taurianova, Reggio Calabria, Italy. They are involved in drug trafficking and have operated in Genoa in the 1990s. The DIA describes them as one of the most traditional and high-profile criminal organizations in the Taurianova and Cittanova area. They are allied with the Piromalli, Zappia, and Gullace-Raso-Albanese clans.

Badr Organization.description: Iraqi Shia Islamist political party and paramilitary organization, formed in 1982 as the military wing of the Supreme Council for Islamic Revolution in Iraq (SCIRI). Based in Iran during the Iran-Iraq War, it fought the Ba'athist regime. After 2003, it became a political party and its fighters joined Iraqi security forces. It remains active as part of the Popular Mobilization Forces.

descriptions: Badr Organization fought against ISIL in 2014.
edges.evidence_quote: Badr Brigade forces, and their Iranian commanders, have come to prominence in 2014 fighting the Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant (ISIL) in Iraq.

descriptions: Both Badr Brigade and Mahdi Army were accused of carrying out sectarian killings.
edges.evidence_quote: Another was the Mahdi Army of the young cleric Muqtada al-Sadr, who is now part of the Shia coalition seeking to form a government after winning the mid-December election.

North Coast Cartel.description: The North Coast Cartel was a Colombian drug cartel operating along the Caribbean coast from the 1980s to 2010/2011. Based in Barranquilla, it controlled drug trafficking routes and local production. Its leader Alberto Orlandez Gamboa was arrested in 1998 and extradited to the US. The cartel was involved in drug trafficking, money laundering, and violence.

descriptions: Norte del Valle Cartel killed members of the North Coast Cartel (Gustavo and Fernando Salazar Bernal).
edges.evidence_quote: Gustavo Salazar Bernal: Killed in Cartagena on August 30, 2001, by gunman Jhon Fredy Orrego Marín who was associated to the Norte del Valle Cartel . He was killed for being responsible for "losing" a shipment of drugs property of "Los Mellizos" (The Twins) with destiny to Europe. His brother Fernando Salazar Bernal was also killed by the Norte del Valle Cartel .

descriptions: Los Mellizos had a drug shipment lost by a North Coast Cartel member, leading to retaliation.
edges.evidence_quote: Gustavo Salazar Bernal: Killed in Cartagena on August 30, 2001, by gunman Jhon Fredy Orrego Marín who was associated to the Norte del Valle Cartel . He was killed for being responsible for "losing" a shipment of drugs property of "Los Mellizos" (The Twins) with destiny to Europe.

descriptions: North Coast Cartel hired AUC for protection of drug routes and labs.
edges.evidence_quote: The cartel is believed to have employed the services of the United Self-Defense Forces of Colombia (AUC), a right-wing paramilitary organization internationally classified as a terrorist organization, to protect the cartel's drug routes, its drug laboratories, and its members and associates.

descriptions: Sinaloa Cartel influenced cocaine trafficking from Colombia, likely involving North Coast Cartel routes.
edges.evidence_quote: Ismael 'El Mayo' Zambada ... conceded in court how the Sinaloa Cartel held major influence over the trafficking of cocaine which originated in Colombia.

Greek mafia.description: The Greek mafia is a colloquial term for various large-scale organized crime elements originating in Greece or operated by Greeks. These groups are involved in racketeering, smuggling, money laundering, drug trafficking, kidnapping, and murder. They operate primarily in Greek urban centers like Athens, often as family-based cells, and collaborate with Albanian, Romanian, and Serbian mafia groups. Greek crime bosses are known as 'godfathers of the night' and often run illegal businesses from nightclubs.
